title: Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 8.2.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 8.2.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.42: Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.42' running on index=netfw GlobalProtect or sourcetype="pan:globalprotect"; index=itsm snow:sc_req_item, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928)
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT cde_profile
| where cde_profile="true"
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-30d short_description="*VPN*CDE*"
    | eval user=lower(caller_id)
    | stats latest(state) as req_state by user
  ]
| where isnull(req_state) OR NOT match(req_state,"(?i)closed|fulfilled")
| stats dc(_time) as connect_events by user, source
| sort - connect_events
description: Verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm, netfw and sourcetypes pan:globalprotect, snow:sc_req_item. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize `user` to corporate UPN. (2) Align RITM naming with join filter. (3) Alert IAM on missing ticket within 24h of first connect. (4) Monthly reconciliation report. Indexes required: `index=itsm`, `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`, `sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item`; Lookups: `pci_cde_vpn_profiles`, `pci_cde_vpn_profiles.csv`.
visualization: Table (users without tickets), column chart (connect_events), pie (fulfilled vs missing).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Firewall changes during scheduled change windows, controlled segmentation rollouts, or planned audits.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` GlobalProtect or `sourcetype="pan:globalprotect"`; `index=itsm` `snow:sc_req_item`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `user` to corporate UPN. (2) Align RITM naming with join filter. (3) Alert IAM on missing ticket within 24h of first connect. (4) Monthly reconciliation report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT cde_profile
| where cde_profile="true"
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-30d short_description="*VPN*CDE*"
    | eval user=lower(caller_id)
    | stats latest(state) as req_state by user
  ]
| where isnull(req_state) OR NOT match(req_state,"(?i)closed|fulfilled")
| stats dc(_time) as connect_events by user, source
| sort - connect_events
```

Understanding this SPL

**Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts (PCI DSS Req 7.2.3, 8.2.1)** — Verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` GlobalProtect or `sourcetype="pan:globalprotect"`; `index=itsm` `snow:sc_req_item`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where cde_profile="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **user** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(req_state) OR NOT match(req_state,"(?i)closed|fulfilled")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **user, source**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts (PCI DSS Req 7.2.3, 8.2.1)** — Verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` GlobalProtect or `sourcetype="pan:globalprotect"`; `index=itsm` `snow:sc_req_item`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: connect_events, req_state, source, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (users without tickets), column chart (connect_events), pie (fulfilled vs missing).
grandmaExplanation: We verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
